issue.title: Bug: Ctrl+C and Ctrl+V are completely non-functional in the terminal

phases.title: Restore Ctrl+V paste and add Ctrl+C copy/SIGINT in Terminal.tsx (no-plugin path)
verification: Terminal.tsx `attachCustomKeyEventHandler` has a new `Ctrl+C` branch: copies via navigator.clipboard.writeText and returns false when xterm.getSelection() is non-empty; returns true (SIGINT passthrough) when there is no selection.
Terminal.tsx has a `Ctrl+V` / `Ctrl+Shift+V` branch that reads the clipboard and calls xterm.paste(), returning false.
A capture-phase `paste` listener is registered on `container` after xterm.open() and removed in the effect cleanup ŌĆö registered with the `true` (capture) flag in both add and remove.
The existing Ctrl+Shift+C copy branch is unchanged.
No changes to Cargo.toml, package.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, or capabilities/*.json.
`pnpm build` from the repo root passes (tsc -b + vite build) ŌĆö clean TypeScript, no unused-symbol errors.
Manual smoke (user, before tag push): Ctrl+V pastes clipboard content exactly once ŌĆö verified with a 200-char single line and a 5-line block.
Manual smoke (user): Ctrl+C with an active selection copies and does NOT interrupt; Ctrl+C with no selection sends SIGINT and interrupts a running process.
Manual smoke (user): Ctrl+Shift+C still copies the selection.
